title: Selliguea dareiformis
intro: Selliguea dareiformis, also known as Polypodium dareiformis, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Selliguea dareiformis include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Selliguea dareiformis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Selliguea dareiformis belongs to the family Polypodiaceae, which is a large family of ferns commonly known as the spleenworts. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf structure and spore production. The genus Selliguea is a part of the Polypodiaceae family and is native to tropical regions. Within the Polypodiaceae family, Selliguea dareiformis is classified as a member of the subfamily Polypodioideae.
